rag_info.chunk_content: Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, Lotus Technology has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned, thereunto duly authorized. NEW YORK, Jan. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the full year of 2024. Lotus Technology delivered 12,065 vehicles in 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of over 70% and boasting the top growth rate among leading luxury brands. China contributed approximately 25% of the total global deliveries in 2024. In 2025, Lotus Technology aims to increase global deliveries by 20% with a 25-28% contribution from China. Lotus Technology also successfully reclaimed the proprietary rights of the LOTUS roundel, LOTUS word mark, and the Chinese character of Lotus ("莲花") in China, achieving consistency in terms of its trademark across the world. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market. The vehicle delivery numbers presented above are derived from reports submitted by Lotus Technology's business units. These numbers may differ slightly from the vehicle delivery numbers recorded in the financial accounting and reporting systems announced or to be announced by Lotus Technology. Note 2: Including Ferrari, Porsche, Aston Martin, Bentley, Rolls Royce, and McLaren.

rag_info.chunk_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports cars for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. Lotus Technology has established a successful track record since its inception. In 2024, Lotus Technology delivered a total of 12,134 vehicles, which consisted of its lifestyle vehicles and sportscars distributed through Lotus Technology International Limited (LTIL). As of December 31, 2024, Lotus Technology had 1,996 full-time employees globally, including in China and Europe, among which approximately 52% belong to its R&D division. Lotus Technology's total revenues increased from US\$9.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2022, to US\$679.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, and again to US\$924.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Lotus Technology recorded a net loss of US\$724.6 million, US\$750.3 million, and US\$1,107.3 million in 2022, 2023, and 2024, respectively.

title: Lotus Technology Inc. Unaudited Condensed Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive loss 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has reported its financial performance for the years ending December 31, 2024, and 2023, highlighting significant changes in revenue, costs, and losses. In 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $924.3 million, a notable increase from $679 million in 2023, primarily due to higher sales of goods and services. However, the cost of revenues also surged to $894.7 million from $576.8 million, resulting in a gross profit decline to $29.6 million from $102.2 million the previous year.

Operating expenses decreased slightly to $815.9 million in 2024 from $838.1 million in 2023, with reductions in research and development and selling and marketing costs. Despite these efforts, the operating loss grew to $786.3 million, up from $735.9 million in 2023. The company also faced increased interest expenses, leading to a net loss before income taxes of $1.1 billion in 2024, compared to $749.1 million in the prior year. The overall net loss for 2024 reached $1.1 billion, an increase from $750.3 million in 2023, with the loss per ordinary share rising to ($1.72) from ($1.60).

These results indicate that while Lotus Technology experienced substantial revenue growth, it also encountered rising costs and deepening losses, reflecting ongoing challenges. All figures are in thousands of US dollars, and adjustments for recapitalization following a merger in February 2024 have been made.

summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Revenues for Lotus Technology Inc.**

For the six months ended June 30, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. achieved total revenues of $398,115, marking a substantial increase from $130,035 during the same period in 2023. This growth is attributed to significant advancements in both product sales and service offerings, categorized into sales to third parties and related parties.

**Sales to Third Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Revenue surged to $262,849 in 2024, up from $72,790 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Sales rose to $102,618 in 2024, compared to $49,146 in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $15,096 in 2024 from $2,111 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Third Parties:** Totaled $380,563 in 2024, a significant rise from $124,047 in 2023.

**Sales to Related Parties:**
- **BEV Lifestyle Models:** Generated $535 in 2024, slightly up from $436 in 2023.
- **Sports Cars:** Recorded sales of $91 in 2024, with no sales in 2023.
- **Other Sales:** Increased to $1,704 in 2024 from $371 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Related Parties:** Reached $2,330 in 2024, compared to $807 in 2023.

**Combined Sales Subtotal:** The total revenue from goods sold to both third and related parties was $382,893 in 2024, a notable increase from $124,854 in 2023.

**Services Provided:**
- **R&D Services to Third Parties:** Generated $56 in 2024, with no revenue in 2023.
- **Other Services to Third Parties:** Valued at $4,504 in 2024, compared to $160 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Third Parties:** Totaled $4,560 in 2024, up from $160 in 2023.
- **R&D Services to Related Parties:** Increased to $6,366 in 2024 from $4,983 in 2023.
- **Other Services to Related Parties:** Rose to $4,296 in 2024 from $38 in 2023.
- **Subtotal for Services to Related Parties:** Reached $10,662 in 2024, compared to $5,021 in 2023.

**Total Services Subtotal:** The combined revenue from services provided to both third and related parties was $15,222 in 2024, up from $5,181 in 2023.

This comprehensive revenue analysis underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust growth across various product and service lines, reflecting its expanding market presence and improved operational performance. All figures are presented in thousands of US dollars, with footnotes clarifying additional service categories.

Additionally, non-GAAP measures were adjusted for non-deductible share-based compensation expenses. metadata.title_summary:
title: 27. REVENUES 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated substantial revenue growth across its various service lines, as detailed in the "Revenue Breakdown by Service Line" table for the years ending December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022. The company's revenues are segmented into sales to third parties and related parties, providing insights into the performance of each area. In 2024, Lotus reported total revenues of $924.3 million, a significant increase from $679.0 million in 2023 and just $9.6 million in 2022.

The company's standout performance was in the sales of battery electric vehicle (BEV) lifestyle models, which generated $627.7 million in 2024, up from $464.2 million in 2023, with no revenue recorded in 2022. Sales of sports cars also rose to $211.8 million in 2024, compared to $183.2 million the previous year. Total sales of goods to third parties reached $872.9 million in 2024, while related party sales contributed an additional $18.2 million, bringing the subtotal for total goods sold to $891.1 million.

Service revenues presented a mixed picture, with R&D services to third parties declining to $1.2 million, while other services increased to $7.7 million. Overall, total service revenue from third parties was $8.9 million, and related party services generated $24.4 million, culminating in total service revenue of $33.3 million in 2024. The "Others" category includes a variety of services such as training programs, installation of intelligent driving systems, maintenance services, and vehicle internet connection services, while "2 Others" primarily consists of commission service fees. This comprehensive breakdown underscores Lotus Technology Inc.'s robust performance and growth trajectory across its service lines.

title: Eletre 
summary: The Lotus Eletre and Emeya represent the brand's commitment to innovative electric vehicle design, combining luxury, performance, and sustainability. The Eletre is a pioneering pure electric SUV that sets a new standard for lifestyle vehicles, featuring a spacious interior for five passengers, customizable driving modes, and three distinct versions—Eletre, Eletre S, and Eletre R—catering to different customer preferences. The Eletre models are equipped with advanced powertrains, impressive ranges, and rapid charging capabilities, alongside a focus on aerodynamics and luxurious interiors enhanced by cutting-edge technology, including a sophisticated infotainment system and 5G connectivity.

In contrast, the Emeya is Lotus's first hyper grand tourer, showcasing a sleek design and high-performance metrics, aimed at redefining the electric vehicle experience. Deliveries of the Emeya began in China in March 2024, with plans for European deliveries later that year, and it also carries a price point exceeding $100,000. Both models reflect Lotus's dedication to eco-friendly practices and advanced technology, while the broader vision for future models emphasizes the integration of sustainable practices and smart technologies to address urban challenges and enhance quality of life.

title: Eletre 
summary: The Eletre represents Lotus's first foray into the electric vehicle market as a pure electric SUV and lifestyle vehicle, measuring 5,103 mm in length with a 3,019 mm wheelbase, prioritizing spaciousness and comfort. It features five distinct drive modes that can be adjusted through various vehicle systems, emphasizing aerodynamics with a design that enhances airflow to minimize resistance. The interior is equipped with durable materials and a 15.1-inch OLED screen, alongside a digital passenger display and an augmented reality (AR) head-up display (HUD). The vehicle operates on Lotus Hyper OS, which provides advanced digital mapping and connectivity, including 5G compatibility for over-the-air updates. Safety features include collision mitigation, traffic alerts, and lane departure warnings.

Customers have the option to cancel their orders; however, deposits are generally non-refundable under specific conditions. The Eletre is projected to achieve an annual sales volume of 40,000 to 50,000 units starting in 2026.

In addition to the Eletre, Lotus is set to launch the Emeya (Type 133), its second lifestyle vehicle and first hyper grand tourer, built on the same architecture as the Eletre. The Emeya boasts impressive performance, accelerating from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.8 seconds. It features supercharging capabilities, allowing for a range of 150 km in five minutes and achieving 80% capacity in 18 minutes with a 350 kW charger. Deliveries of the Emeya commenced in China in March 2024, with European deliveries anticipated in the second half of 2024. The average Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the Emeya exceeds $100,000, with expected annual sales of 30,000 to 40,000 units starting in 2028.

Overall, both the Eletre and Emeya highlight Lotus's commitment to innovation in the electric vehicle segment, combining advanced technology, performance, and luxury in their designs.
